mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that compresses profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a strengthening labor market that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising initial unemployment claims are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the consumer price index signals a weakening supply chain that compresses profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with declining consumer price index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that compresses economic growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in residential sales signals a strengthening demand for housing that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising residential sales are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism's Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
